evidence_boundary: These examples prove schema conformance and selection reasoning only; they are not RF performance or regulatory evidence.

examples.mission_profile: Planned low-rate sensor telemetry to a private site gateway

selection_rationale.privacy_and_security: unique OTAA roots plus signed application envelope
selection_rationale.regulation: implementation must record current local profile
selection_rationale.available_hardware: gateway and endpoints are implementation choices
selection_rationale.rf_coexistence: airtime budget is reserved from human traffic

rejected_alternatives.reason: shared flooding is unnecessary for the planned machine topology
rejected_alternatives.reconsider_when: the gateway is unavailable and an ad-hoc human path is required

known_gaps: exact regional parameters and network-server build are not selected

examples.mission_profile: Rapidly formed mobile search team carrying phones and LoRa companions

selection_rationale.mission: brief text, status, SOS and location
selection_rationale.privacy_and_security: known default keys forbidden and app signature authoritative
selection_rationale.available_hardware: reusable compatible nodes may be used
selection_rationale.rf_coexistence: congestion threshold must be measured before field use

rejected_alternatives.reason: no planned repeater placement in this rapid-start scenario
rejected_alternatives.reconsider_when: repeaters can be surveyed and installed

known_gaps: physical congestion, range and current firmware compatibility remain unverified

examples.mission_profile: Planned urban response cell with strategically placed repeaters

selection_rationale.mission: human team messaging across obstructed sectors
selection_rationale.density: multiple teams with separated channels and budgets
selection_rationale.privacy_and_security: OpenBREC signature and incident identities remain authoritative
selection_rationale.regulation: incident command assumes a bounded documented uncertainty
selection_rationale.rf_coexistence: path and airtime monitoring trigger shutdown

rejected_alternatives.reason: planned repeaters reduce routine flood forwarding in this scenario

assumed_risk_decision.antenna: recorded model gain and feed loss
assumed_risk_decision.reason: immediate life-safety communication need with no validated alternative
assumed_risk_decision.monitoring: airtime, interference and message health continuously reviewed
assumed_risk_decision.stop_condition: validated alternative available, interference threshold exceeded or incident closed
assumed_risk_decision.kill_switch: local physical TX disable at every repeater

known_gaps: example records do not authorize real transmission or select a jurisdictional frequency

examples.mission_profile: Expert-operated heterogeneous federation between isolated response cells

selection_rationale.mission: route authenticated summaries over heterogeneous interfaces
selection_rationale.payload_and_latency: small summaries with occasional larger artifacts
selection_rationale.privacy_and_security: incident authorization remains above Reticulum identity
selection_rationale.rf_coexistence: interface choice occurs after application priority

rejected_alternatives.reason: heterogeneous peer routing is needed rather than a single network-server domain
rejected_alternatives.reconsider_when: a planned machine-only star topology is sufficient

known_gaps: selected Reticulum version, interfaces and operator SOP remain unverified

examples.mission_profile: Long partition reconciled by a physically carried encrypted bundle

selection_rationale.mission: preserve and reconcile delayed summaries and evidence
selection_rationale.payload_and_latency: larger payload and high tolerated delay
selection_rationale.privacy_and_security: encrypted signed bundle with custody record

rejected_alternatives.reconsider_when: a heterogeneous live path is restored

known_gaps: physical custody and removable-media procedure require implementation evidence
